paragraphs.text: Meet the operations leaders, logistics engineers, and policy voices shaping the next era of North American industrial resilience. The 2025 program features twelve confirmed speakers across three days in Pittsburgh.
Dr. Reyes leads federal industrial resilience strategy across 14 priority supply corridors. Her work redefined the national framework for critical-component sourcing after 2022.
James oversees one of the largest intermodal networks in the Pacific Northwest, moving more than 9 million tons annually. He is a frequent advisor to congressional logistics committees.
Aisha has rebuilt Ferrocept's transcontinental rail-to-port pipeline over the last four years. She regularly publishes on multi-modal redundancy planning.
Brendan authored the council's 2024 white paper on reshoring incentives for mid-tier metal fabricators. He briefs state legislators monthly on workforce policy.
Sofia runs three mills across western Pennsylvania producing structural steel for infrastructure clients. She championed Keystone's transition to predictive maintenance in 2023.
Daniel manages component sourcing across 22 countries for industrial automation systems. His team pioneered dual-source contracts for semiconductor-grade actuators.
Elena designs grid-scale energy systems for retrofitted manufacturing campuses. She holds nine patents in distributed industrial power balancing.
Robert coordinates infrastructure policy among 38 member ports along the eastern seaboard. He testified before the Senate Commerce Committee in 2024.
Linh transformed Monongahela's tier-2 supplier vetting process, cutting onboarding from 90 to 14 days. She mentors emerging procurement professionals in the region.
Gregory designs cross-border freight routing systems serving more than 400 industrial clients. He led Beacon's response to the 2023 Midwest rail disruptions.
Priya studies the intersection of trade policy and regional manufacturing capacity. Her recent book examines four decades of Rust Belt reinvention.
Marcus oversees safety, throughput, and regulatory compliance across six chemical plants. He chairs the regional industrial mutual-aid committee.
Ironclad Futures Summit425 Smithfield StreetPittsburgh, PA 15222

visibleText: IRONCLAD FUTURES program speakers venue register about Speakers Meet the operations leaders, logistics engineers, and policy voices shaping the next era of North American industrial resilience. The 2025 program features twelve confirmed speakers across three days in Pittsburgh. Keynote Speakers MR KEYNOTE Dr. Margaret Reyes Chief Resilience Officer, U.S. Manufacturing Consortium Dr. Reyes leads federal industrial resilience strategy across 14 priority supply corridors. Her work redefined the national framework for critical-component sourcing after 2022. JT KEYNOTE James Tolliver CEO, Cascadia Freight Systems James oversees one of the largest intermodal networks in the Pacific Northwest, moving more than 9 million tons annually. He is a frequent advisor to congressional logistics committees. Featured Speakers AN Aisha Nakamura Director of Global Logistics at Ferrocept Industries Aisha has rebuilt Ferrocept's transcontinental rail-to-port pipeline over the last four years. She regularly publishes on multi-modal redundancy planning. BC Brendan Cho Senior Policy Analyst at the Allegheny Manufacturing Council Brendan authored the council's 2024 white paper on reshoring incentives for mid-tier metal fabricators. He briefs state legislators monthly on workforce policy. SP Sofia Patel VP of Operations at Keystone Steelworks Sofia runs three mills across western Pennsylvania producing structural steel for infrastructure clients. She championed Keystone's transition to predictive maintenance in 2023. DO Daniel Okafor Head of Supply Chain at Northbridge Robotics Daniel manages component sourcing across 22 countries for industrial automation systems. His team pioneered dual-source contracts for semiconductor-grade actuators. EM Elena Marchetti Principal Engineer at Rust Belt Energy Solutions Elena designs grid-scale energy systems for retrofitted manufacturing campuses. She holds nine patents in distributed industrial power balancing. RW Robert Whitfield Federal Liaison, North American Ports Alliance Robert coordinates infrastructure policy among 38 member ports along the eastern seaboard. He testified before the Senate Commerce Committee in 2024. LH Linh Hoang Director of Procurement at Monongahela Materials Linh transformed Monongahela's tier-2 supplier vetting process, cutting onboarding from 90 to 14 days. She mentors emerging procurement professionals in the region. GS Gregory Sandoval Logistics Architect at Beacon Cargo Networks Gregory designs cross-border freight routing systems serving more than 400 industrial clients. He led Beacon's response to the 2023 Midwest rail disruptions. PK Priya Krishnan Research Fellow at Carnegie Industrial Policy Institute Priya studies the intersection of trade policy and regional manufacturing capacity. Her recent book examines four decades of Rust Belt reinvention. He chairs the regional industrial mutual-aid committee.
